title: Termination
text: This Agreement may be terminated: (a) by either Party by providing fifteen (15) days' prior written notice after expiry of the lock-in period, if any; (b) by the Licensor/Landlord immediately if the Licensee/Tenant fails to pay the license fee or rent within fifteen (15) days of the due date on two or more consecutive occasions, carries on any illegal or immoral activity on the Premises, or commits any material breach that remains unremedied for seven (7) days after written notice. Upon termination, the Licensee/Tenant shall vacate the Premises within the notice period, return them in the same condition as at commencement (reasonable wear and tear excepted), and hand over all keys and access devices. The Licensor/Landlord may seek possession through appropriate legal proceedings if the Licensee/Tenant fails to vacate.
statutory_reference: Transfer of Property Act, 1882 – S.106; Model Tenancy Act, 2021 – S.21
name: Termination

legal_basis.note: Notice for termination — 15 days for month-to-month

jurisdiction_constraints.general: TPA S.106 — 15 days notice for month-to-month; Rent Control Acts override in controlled premises
jurisdiction_constraints.Maharashtra: Maharashtra Rent Control Act 1999 applies to older premises — eviction restricted to specific grounds

dispute_hotspots: Eviction without following TPA S.106 notice period — unlawful eviction claim
Rent Control Act protection — tenant in controlled premises cannot be evicted without court order
Wrongful termination during lock-in period — penalty clause validity under ICA S.74
ICA S.23 — termination clause was previously triggering false positive on 'unlawful purpose' — fixed in v3.0
